headline: An RFC 3161 timestamp parser joins the pki.schema family.
summary: pki.schema.tsp parses RFC 3161 Time-Stamp Protocol structures from DER or PEM. parse decodes a TimeStampResp — the message a client receives from a TSA — and enforces the status-to-token coupling: a granted response carries a timestamp token, a rejection carries only a status and its decoded PKIFailureInfo bits. A timestamp token is itself a CMS SignedData whose encapsulated content is a TSTInfo, so parseToken composes the CMS parser, asserts the id-ct-TSTInfo content type, the attached content, and the single-signer rule (RFC 3161 §2.4.2), then decodes the inner TSTInfo. parseTstInfo decodes a bare TSTInfo payload directly. The parser surfaces everything a verifier needs and interprets nothing it cannot: the messageImprint hash algorithm and raw digest, the genTime as a Date with sub-second precision, the serialNumber and nonce losslessly (BigInt plus hex), the accuracy (seconds / millis / micros), and the policy. The imprint-to-request and nonce-to-request round-trips, the ESS signing-certificate binding, and the timestamping EKU are verification-layer concerns above parse altitude. DER-only, fail-closed.

Added: pki.schema.tsp — an RFC 3161 timestamp parser. pki.schema.tsp.parse turns a DER Buffer or PEM into a TimeStampResp ({ status, statusString, failInfo, timeStampToken }) with the granted-carries-token / rejected-carries-none coupling enforced (tsp/missing-token, tsp/unexpected-token) and the PKIFailureInfo named bits decoded. pki.schema.tsp.parseToken parses a TimeStampToken by composing pki.schema.cms.parse and asserting the id-ct-TSTInfo content type (tsp/wrong-econtent-type), attached content (tsp/detached-token), and the single (TSA) signer (tsp/multi-signer), returning the decoded TSTInfo plus the signer material. pki.schema.tsp.parseTstInfo decodes a bare TSTInfo. The TSTInfo mandatory version-1, the GeneralizedTime-only genTime, the accuracy 1..999 range, the ordering DEFAULT FALSE omission, and the PKIStatus 0..5 range are all enforced fail-closed. pki.schema.parse detect-and-routes a TimeStampResp.

The codec and schema engine gain three composable primitives that TSP required: pki.asn1.read.integerImplicit / pki.schema.engine.implicitInteger(tag) (a context-tagged IMPLICIT INTEGER, for the Accuracy millis / micros fields); pki.schema.engine.implicitSeqOf(tag, item) (an order-preserving IMPLICIT SEQUENCE OF, the sibling of implicitSetOf without the SET ordering rule, for the extensions field); and RFC 3161 / X.690 §11.7 fractional-seconds GeneralizedTime support in pki.asn1.read.time (a '.'-separated, trailing-zero-free, Z-terminated fraction, surfaced at millisecond precision).

The OID registry gains the id-kp extended-key-purpose family (including id-kp-timeStamping) and the id-aa S/MIME authenticated-attribute family (signingCertificate / signingCertificateV2 / timeStampToken), so a parsed TSA certificate's key purpose and a signer's ESS binding attribute resolve by name.
